titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Monacensis
VL13
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Jo Lc Mc

decoDesc.p: Black ink with rubrication and other decoration

listBibl.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE. Digitised microfilm is available online at http://daten.digitale-sammlungen.de/~db/bsb00006573/images

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 81v-82r(a fragment with part of John) and ff. 203r-251v. Lacunose in John 10:11-12:38, 21:9-17, 18-20.The transcription includes foliation, lineation, punctuation, digraphs and abbreviations. It is not always possible to make out rubrication from the black and white images. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Correction to John 4:36, and change to verse division in 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: It is possible that this word was written with a Greek letter pi in place of 'P', like the preceding word 'explicit'.
A decorative line is followed by a picture of two birds.
The text of John in sequence continues with John 1:7 on Folio 203r.
This page appears out of the gospel sequence.
Decoration underneath of picture of a tree with birds.
Possible erasure of 'in' in this gap.
The corrector may have changed the first letter of this line from 'r' to 'b'.
Staurogram added by a later hand.
sic
Gap in parchment apparently caused by erasure on recto making it impossible to write.
Two lectionary notes in bottom margin in a later hand: 'lectiones que debeant legi per ui domenecas'; 'lectio prima domeneca'.
Lectionary indication in cursive hand in top left corner: 'lectio tercia in die sc¬o epefania'
Possible erasure at the end of 'tibi'.
Possible erasure or hole in parchment (see also recto).
sic
ab.w:
fue
iohannem
Lectionary note in later hand in top left corner: 'lectio prima ad fontes'.
rit

ab.note: Unreadable characters, possibly deleted; perhaps a lectionary indication?.
Lectionary indication in margin by later hand: 'in uigiliis pasce'.
Cross in margin reaching into line; possibly an addition which has been subsequently erased.
Lectionary indication in bottom margin in later hand: 'seconda domeneca lectionem que debeat legi'.
Lectionary note in margin by a later hand: 'tercia dominici porpurea'.
Lectionary indication by later hand in top left margin: 'lectio seconda ad fontes'.
Lectionary indication in left margin by later hand: 'in uigiliis pasce'.
Lectionary note by later hand in margin: 'finem'.
Staurogram added by a later hand.
Lectionary indication by later hand in top left of page: 'lectio in albas die sabbatorum'.
Possible erased character or damage to parchment after 'surge'.
Lectionary indication by later hand: 'finit'.
'ei' is written in slightly larger script.
Hole in parchment.
sic
No running title
Decorative pattern to end of line.
Lectionary indication by later hand at top of page, cut off when manuscript rebound: [P]ost ascensione dn¬i lectio euangelii ...(18 illegible characters)...ecorum.
The two 'n' in 'in nouissima' are a ligature.
Staurogram added by a later hand.
sic
Lectionary indication in bottom margin by a later hand: 'mediantem penteconste lectio euangelii secondum iahanne'.
sic
The ink on the first eleven lines of this column has been absorbed to a greater extent than usual, as if it had been erased and rewritten, but there is no trace of any other text.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
sic
Lectionary indication in later hand across foot of page: "Post medianæm (?) pentecoste sequenti domeneca lectio euangeli secondum iahanne de cece".
This space may be an erasure.
'et procedent' (sic) is added in the margin by a later hand, probably the same which has added the lectionary indications.
The lectionary indication 'finet' has been added by a later hand.
There is a hole in the parchment in the middle of 'haberetis'.
The manuscript is not extant between John 10:11 and 12:38. The current folio numbering runs continuously.
The manuscript is not extant between John 10:11 and 12:38. The current folio numbering runs continuously.
Hole in the parchment in middle of 'honorem'.
There is a flaw in the parchment in the middle of 'spernit'.
Damage to parchment in middle of 'accepit'.
Hole in parchment.
There is a possible addition of an 'e' (to read 'aceersiam') above the line, although the more expected correction would be to 'accersiam'.
flourish
decorated on either side
Decoration of little birds on either side.
A later hand has added a cross or staurogram here, with a lectionary indication in the bottom margin: "In penteconste"
The sequential text of this verse appears on Folio 240 rb
This is a fragmentary leaf inserted after Folio 239v, in the middle of John 16:7.
The amount of space between the visible letters and column beginning implies that line 4 contained more than just 'cingebas'. Juelicher also gives 'praecingebas'.
The text of John 21 continues on Folio 82r (also out of sequence).
The text of this verse continues from Folio 239v
Hole in parchment.
Small hole in parchment.
No running title visible
'quos' is made up of an 'o' written above a 'us' digraph.
Hole in parchment.
The initial 'c' of 'cognoui' looks like a 'g', but this may be a trace of ink.
No running title visible
The reason for the gap in 'principibus' is unclear; it may be that the parchment is particularly thin at this point.
The intention seems to have been to correct 'non' to 'nonne', but the final 'e' was never added.
The word 'esset' is also written faintly in the top margin next to the running title.
No running title

There is a line above the 'e' of 'accepit' which may be a correction of an 'i' to read 'accipit'. However, the ink is darker on the other side of the page, where it seems to be an impression from the verso opposite.
decorative arrangement of dots
sic
No running title
It is possible that there has been an attempt to erase 'quoniam', but ink decay seems more likely.
sic
Lectionary indication by later hand in top margin: "lectio in domeneca in prima pasca".
sic
Lectionary indication above this column in a later hand: "L(ec)tio inpleta [?; the third letter has been corrected] pasca die dominica [final word illegible]".
A cross has been added here in a later hand, referring to the lectionary indication above the column.

Column 1 of Folio 250v looks distinctly different on the page. It appears to have been rewritten by the first hand (or very similar) in darker, larger letters. There is a hanging letter 'T' beginning line 2, and various other letters visible, but we have not been able to to identify the text which was originally written. For ease of display, the text of this column has not been recorded as a correction, apart from the erased 'misit' on line 1.
The text of John 21:8 continues on Folio 240ra, a fragmentary leaf out of sequence.
